eraMap.title: The xDrive30d THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: 3.0L straight-six diesel — 245 bhp, 540 Nm; rear-mounted timing chain is the critical weakness
Revised N57 — 258 bhp, 560 Nm; improved timing chain design; EGR/DPF issues remain
Modular B57 engine — 265–286 bhp, 620 Nm; chain design improved; DPF and EGR are the primary maintenance watch items

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: X6 xDrive30d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Essential maintenance — catch it before catastrophic failure
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair
Standard maintenance on any modern diesel
Viable repair on a well-maintained vehicle

buyingChecks.title: Buying This xDrive30d? Check These First
buyIf: * G06 (2019+) with the B57 engine (modular design, improved reliability)
* Post-2014 F16 with N57TU (improved timing chain design)
* Documented timing chain history on any N57-engined car
* Full service history with regular oil changes (critical for chain and bearing longevity)
* Evidence of regular longer runs (DPF health)
avoidIf: * Pre-2014 N57 with cold-start rattle and no chain history — assume it's overdue
* No service history — the N57 is unforgiving of neglect
* Short-journey ownership history — DPF and EGR issues likely
* Unexplained coolant loss — may indicate EGR cooler or head gasket issues
* Any X6 with a history of timing chain failure — walk away

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The X6 xDrive30d is BMW's 3.0-litre straight-six diesel flagship across the Sports Activity Coupé range — offering effortless performance, refinement and economy in a distinctive coupe-SUV package. The N57 (E71, F16) is a powerful and efficient engine with one critical weakness — the rear-mounted timing chain. Post-2014 N57TU versions are improved, but chain maintenance remains important. The B57 (G06, 2019+) is the most refined and reliable, with the chain design improved. All xDrive30d engines share DPF and EGR maintenance requirements. With typical X6 values ranging from £10,000 (E71) to £40,000+ (G06), an engine replacement is a significant investment — the B57-engined G06 is the safest choice, but a well-maintained N57TU can also serve well.

cta.label: → Compare xDrive30d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X6 xDrive30d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling from the rear of the engine (N57 timing chain warning) — see the map above
* Service History — Including which engine era (N57D30O0, N57D30A or B57D30O0) and oil-change discipline
* Timing Chain — For N57 (E71/F16): check for any chain work or cold-start rattle
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate wear on all diesel eras
* EGR System — Look for signs of carbon buildup or coolant loss; any performance issues may indicate EGR problems
* Turbo Health — Test acceleration; any lag, whistling, or hesitation?
* Oil Condition — Check for any metallic particles (bearing wear warning)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes read; unresolved issues may indicate DPF, EGR or timing problems
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.question: How much does a BMW X6 xDrive30d engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned xDrive30d engine typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only, with the B57 variant at the higher end. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500 depending on the specialist. A BMW X6 xDrive30d reconditioned engine is available from approximately £1,898. For X-drive models, the cost is generally higher due to drivetrain complexity. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your X6.

items.question: Is the BMW X6 xDrive30d engine reliable?
items.answer: It depends on which generation you have. The N57 (E71, 2008–2014) has a well-documented rear-mounted timing chain stretch risk. The post-2014 N57TU (F16) is improved. The B57 (G06, 2019+) is the most refined and reliable, with the chain design improved. All xDrive30d engines share DPF and EGR maintenance requirements.

items.question: What are the common problems with the BMW X6 xDrive30d?
items.answer: The main issues are timing chain stretch on early N57 engines (rattling from the rear of the engine is the warning sign), DPF clogging — particularly with short journeys, EGR valve and cooler clogging with soot and carbon deposits, turbocharger wear on high-mileage vehicles, and injector failure.

items.question: Is it worth replacing an xDrive30d engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: Depends on the generation and vehicle condition. Against a well-kept G06 worth £25,000–£40,000, a £5,500–£7,500 recon is 15–30% of the car's value — worth it. Against a tired E71 worth £6,000–£10,000, the same recon may approach our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first. B57-engined cars command a premium due to improved reliability.

items.question: How do I find my BMW X6 xDrive30d engine code?
items.answer: E71 (2008–2014): N57D30O0. F16 (2014–2019): N57D30A. G06 (2019–present): B57D30O0. Check the VIN plate or vehicle documentation.

items.question: Can I repair the N57 timing chain, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: The chain and guides must be replaced if stretched or worn. A preventative replacement costs £1,500–£3,500 (engine-out job). A snapped chain almost always destroys the engine, requiring a £4,500–£7,500+ replacement. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.
